Location Viability Index69.8

Bottom line. On the numbers this is a site I'd pursue — a Location Viability Index of 69.8 out of 100 (I'm ~90% confident the true value sits between 56.3 and 78.6). Here's what's underneath that.

Demographics do the heavy lifting (35% of the index). Median household income here is $111,095 and median age 43.1. Your OSA/TMD patients are the affluent, 40-plus, often-cash-pay cohort, and this tract carries the score. Because it's the most heavily weighted factor, a strong reading here is what pulls the whole verdict up.

Referral access is the second engine (25%). I count 64 referring physicians in the catchment — sleep medicine, ENT, neurology, primary care — and 40 are clustered within ~0.2 mi of the site itself. For a referral-fed specialty that's the single most valuable thing a site can offer, which is why a doctor on your block counts for far more here than one across the ZIP.

Competition trims, it doesn't sink (10%, a deduction). There are 5 credentialed orofacial-pain/TMJ/sleep specialists in the trade area, the nearest about 1.1 mi away — near you, but not on top of you. This is a contested market, so the play is differentiation and referral relationships rather than being first; the distance math is measured from each competitor's real street address, not a ZIP approximation.

Two reality checks on top of the index: a gravity model predicts you'd capture ~17.0% of expected demand, or ~9.0% in the opening year before you mature; and unit economics project ~814 cases/yr against the ~172 you need to break even, so it clears break-even with cushion.

What's deliberately not in this number yet. Operations and rent are held neutral until you price a specific suite on the Real Estate tab — so think of 69.8 as the quality of the location, before the economics of any one lease. Drop in a real rent and square footage and the index re-prices for that suite.
